name: Monastery of Bonaval and Medieval Bridge of Valdesotos — Gorges of Jarama
name_original: Monasterio de Bonaval y Puente Medieval de Valdesotos — Hoces del Jarama
region_intro: Hiking around Retiendas offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, characterized by rugged gorges, serene reservoirs, and extensive forests. The region features varied terrain, including paths along the Jarama River and routes through holm oak woodlands. Elevation changes are present, with trails ranging from gentle gradients to more significant ascents. Best hiking trails around Retiendas The most popular hiking route is Monastery of Bonaval and Medieval Bridge of Valdesotos — Gorges of Jarama, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses the Hoces del Jarama, passing historical ruins and a medieval bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Vereda from the El Vado reservoir — circular through the Hoz de Vallosera (PR-GU 18), a moderate 9.1 miles (14.7 km) path. This circular route explores the area around the Embalse de El Vado, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding natural features. Local hikers also love the Arcipreste de Hita Trail, a 9.4 miles (15.2 km) trail leading through the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Retiendas is defined by rugged gorges, serene reservoirs, and forests of holm oaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging treks. The routes in Retiendas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Retiendas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Retiendas?
A: Retiendas offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 35 trails to explore. These routes cater to various preferences, from moderate paths to more challenging treks through the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Retiendas?
A: Yes, Retiendas has several options for easier hikes. While most routes are moderate, there are 2 trails specifically rated as easy, perfect for those seeking a less strenuous experience or hiking with family. These trails often feature gentler gradients and shorter distances.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Retiendas?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Retiendas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022943762/the-vereda-from-the-el-vado-reservoir-circular-through-the-hoz-de-vallosera-pr-gu-18" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Vereda from the El Vado reservoir — circular through the Hoz de Vallosera (PR-GU 18)&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the picturesque area around the Embalse de El Vado.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Retiendas?
A: Hiking in Retiendas offers diverse natural beauty. You'll encounter the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Hoces del Jarama&lt;/strong&gt; (Jarama Gorges) with their high walls and lush vegetation, the serene &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de El Vado&lt;/strong&gt; (El Vado Reservoir), and extensive forests of holm oaks. Keep an eye out for vultures soaring above the gorges.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the trails around Retiendas are rich in history. You can visit the impressive ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022926848/monastery-of-bonaval-and-medieval-bridge-of-valdesotos-gorges-of-jarama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Bonaval&lt;/a&gt;, a Cistercian monastery founded in 1164. Another significant landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022926848/monastery-of-bonaval-and-medieval-bridge-of-valdesotos-gorges-of-jarama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Valdesotos&lt;/a&gt;, offering exceptional views over the Jarama canyon. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillos Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is part of routes showcasing traditional black architecture.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Retiendas?
A: The hiking experience in Retiendas is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural landscapes of the Jarama Gorges, and the historical points of interest like the Bonaval Monastery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Retiendas?
A: The Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, where Retiendas is located, offers pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter can bring colder weather, though trails remain accessible.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Retiendas dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural surroundings with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas or near wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations before you go.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads in Retiendas?
A: Public transport options directly to all trailheads in Retiendas can be limited due to its rural location. It is recommended to check local bus schedules for services to Retiendas or nearby villages, but for ultimate flexibility, traveling by car is often the most convenient way to access various starting points.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Retiendas?
A: Parking is generally available in Retiendas village itself, and at designated points near popular trailheads, such as around the El Vado Reservoir. Look for marked parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival to ensure you park responsibly and without obstructing local access.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views in Retiendas?
A: Yes, the rugged landscape of Retiendas provides several opportunities for stunning views. The trails through the Hoces del Jarama offer exceptional vistas over the canyon, and areas around the El Vado Reservoir provide picturesque outlooks. You can also find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2699850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the reservoir from the Royuelo ravine&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on hikes around Retiendas?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly across trails in Retiendas. While some routes have minimal ascent, others, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022943762/the-vereda-from-the-el-vado-reservoir-circular-through-the-hoz-de-vallosera-pr-gu-18" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Vereda from the El Vado reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, can involve over 400 meters of climbing, requiring good physical condition. There are options for both gentle gradients and more challenging ascents.
tips:
The remains that currently survive date from the 13th century. Various reforms were made in the 17th century and it was finally abandoned by the monks in 1821, passing into private hands. It was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1992, being restored and conditioned for visits by the Castilla-La Mancha Community Board in 2018.
Of the monastic complex, the apse, the sacristy, the transept and the southern nave of the church are preserved, as well as some walls of other dependencies.

La Hoz del Jarama is a strip of land located between the well-known Cistercian Monastery of Bonaval and the Medieval Bridge (which many call “Roman”) of Valdesotos. In this section, the river twists and its bed is sometimes wedged between large walls of limestone rock.

Medieval bridge, with three eyes and a cobbled floor. It is crossed by the old road to Puebla de Valles that climbs between the rocks. Great views of the canyon, the river and the caves. From the bridge, a path goes down to the little beach that can be seen on the right bank, ideal for swimming on a hot day.

waypoints.name: Medieval Bridge over the Jarama River
